_comment: Real FOIA cases cited by the DOJ OIP Guide to the FOIA. Each entry
reproduces a VERBATIM passage from the Guide describing the records
at issue and the court's disposition. scenarios/generate_scenarios.py
consumes this file to emit one scenario JSON per case; a test
(tests/test_cases_grounded.py) asserts every quoted string is a real
substring of the fetched DOJ Guide PDF for its named source+page
Neither the scenarios nor the gold labels are hand-authored content.
They are extracted from published federal guidance, each backed by
a locatable citation (source_doc + source_page) that a reviewer can
open and verify against the original PDF.

cases.case_name: Department of the Interior v. Klamath Water Users Protective Ass'n, 532 U.S. 1 (2001)
cases.record_description_quote: communications between the Department of the Interior and several Indian tribes which, in expressing their views to the Department on certain matters of administrative decisionmaking
cases.outcome_quote: the Court ruled that the threshold of Exemption 5 did not encompass
cases.note: Consultant-corollary limit: parties with their own interests are not 'consultants' for (b)(5) purposes.

cases.case_name: Physicians Committee for Responsible Medicine v. NIH
cases.record_description_quote: documents submitted by an NIH grant applicant
cases.outcome_quote: Exemption 5 could not be used to protect
cases.note: Grant applicants are not 'consultants' under Klamath — (b)(5) does not apply.

cases.record_description_quote: personally identifying information about those individuals
cases.outcome_quote: exempt from disclosure
cases.note: Individuals wrongly labeled as 'businesses' still had (b)(6) privacy interests.

cases.record_description_quote: the release of names and home addresses
cases.outcome_quote: involuntary personal involvement
cases.note: Release of names+home addresses would cause involuntary involvement without furthering any public interest — (b)(6) applies.

cases.case_name: Center for National Security Studies v. DOJ
cases.record_description_quote: the names of post-9/11 detainees, found on documents that traditionally have been public
cases.outcome_quote: properly withheld
cases.note: Even records traditionally public can be (b)(7) when compiled for law-enforcement purpose pursuing a federal-law violation + national security breach.

cases.case_name: National Resources Defense Council v. DOD
cases.record_description_quote: well information pertaining to privately and publicly owned water wells
cases.outcome_quote: the FOIA does not distinguish between
cases.note: (b)(9) covers both private and public well data; statute does not distinguish.
